name: From Murtosa to Ovar — circular route
name_original: De Murtosa a Ovar — percurso circular
description: This 23.1-mile (37.1 km) touring cycling route from Murtosa to Ovar offers moderate difficulty and diverse scenery along the Ria de Aveiro.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Murtosa are characterized by the flat terrain surrounding the Ria de Aveiro lagoon, an extensive coastal wetland system. The region features a network of dedicated cycle paths, including the NaturRia routes, which connect charming quays and offer views of diverse ecosystems. Pine forests and wetlands are common along these trails, with minimal elevation changes making many routes accessible. Best touring cycling routes around Murtosa The most popular touring cycling route is From Murtosa to Ovar — circular route, a 23.1 miles (37.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete, offering a scenic loop between two key towns. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Estarreja and Vouga Valley Ecotrail Loop, a difficult 42.4 miles (68.2 km) path that explores the varied landscapes of the Vouga Valley. Local touring cyclists also love the Great Route of Ria de Aveiro – Boardwalk in Ria de Aveiro loop from Estarreja, a 32.9 miles (52.9 km) trail leading through the unique Ria de Aveiro lagoon landscape, often completed in about 4 hours 20 minutes. Touring cycling around Murtosa is defined by flat lagoon-side paths, traditional quays, and diverse wetlands. The network offers options for cyclists of varying ability levels, from easy family-friendly rides to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Murtosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1,400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Murtosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Murtosa?
A: Murtosa offers nearly 40 touring cycling routes, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for touring cycling routes in Murtosa?
A: You'll find a good mix of routes in Murtosa. Over half of the routes, 21 to be exact, are classified as easy, making them ideal for relaxed rides. There are also 14 moderate routes and 4 more challenging options for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Murtosa?
A: Yes, Murtosa is well-suited for family cycling. Many routes are notably flat, especially those along the Ria de Aveiro lagoon and urban cycle paths, making them accessible for all ages. Over 20 routes are classified as easy, perfect for a leisurely family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Murtosa?
A: While specific dog-friendly designations for cycling routes aren't always explicit, the flat, open nature of many paths along the Ria de Aveiro lagoon and through pine forests makes them generally suitable for cycling with a well-behaved dog on a leash or in a trailer. Always ensure your dog is comfortable and safe.

Q: What kind of landscapes and natural features can I expect to see while cycling in Murtosa?
A: Murtosa's touring cycling routes showcase diverse landscapes, from the serene Ria de Aveiro lagoon with its wetlands and canals to pine forests and riverbanks. You'll encounter charming quays, traditional 'moliceiro' boats, and opportunities for birdwatching. The region's close connection to the lagoon is its defining natural feature.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Murtosa?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Murtosa are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An excellent example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1385045554/from-murtosa-to-ovar-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Murtosa to Ovar — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop between two key towns.

Q: What are some notable attractions or landmarks along the cycling routes?
A: Along your cycling journey, you can explore various cultural and natural highlights. You might encounter traditional fishing docks, like those near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3127791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campground and fishing docks&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the unique experience of seeing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3893934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moliceiro Boats on the Canals of Aveiro&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;EV1 on the Ria de Aveiro&lt;/a&gt; is also a prominent feature, offering scenic views.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Murtosa?
A: The komoot community highly rates the touring cycling experience in Murtosa, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Cyclists frequently praise the flat lagoon-side paths, the extensive network of dedicated cycle paths like the NaturRia routes, and the opportunity to explore traditional quays and diverse wetlands. The accessibility for varying ability levels is also a common highlight.

Q: Is it easy to find parking near the touring cycling routes in Murtosa?
A: Murtosa has made efforts to promote cycling, and as such, you can generally find parking options near popular starting points for cycling routes. Many areas, especially those developed under initiatives like 'Murtosa Ciclável,' are designed with cyclist access in mind, which often includes parking facilities.

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes in Murtosa using public transport?
A: Yes, public transport can be a viable option for reaching some touring cycling routes in Murtosa. The region's focus on sustainable mobility means that key areas and starting points for routes may be accessible via local public transport, though specific bike carriage policies should be checked with the transport provider.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes in Murtosa?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, Murtosa offers routes that cover significant distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32162587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great Route of Ria de Aveiro – Boardwalk in Ria de Aveiro loop from Torreira&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route spanning over 100 km, providing an extensive exploration of the lagoon landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Murtosa?
A: Murtosa's mild climate makes it an appealing destination for touring cycling year-round. The varied terrain, from lagoon-side paths to pine forests, can be enjoyed in most seasons. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for longer rides.
tips: Inaugurated on August 16, 1981, the monument in honor of the Murtosa emigrants, the work of José João Brito, was celebrated with an open mass and a parade by local associations, highlighting the presence of the flags of countries relevant to the Murtoseira emigration. The statue was unveiled by João Campos, Murtosa's oldest emigrant.

Ovar, blessed by nature, presents varied scenarios with the estuary, sea and forest. Highlights include Blue Flag beaches, the biodiversity of the Buçaquinho Environmental Park and outdoor activities such as surfing and stand-up paddleboarding. The cultural richness is evident in the decorative tiles and religious heritage, with emphasis on the Churches of Válega and Cortegaça. The municipality is also known for its vibrant cultural and gastronomic life, including the famous Pão-de-Ló de Ovar.
Traditionally important chapel at the head entrance to the Aveiro lagoon, half-way between Ovar and the Atlantic beaches at Furadouro. Here the EuroVelo 1 coastal bike route turns west to the coast.
Alternative road parallel to EN 109 that offers a few kilometers quieter. Furthermore, it runs through corn fields, making the landscape even more picturesque.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the From Murtosa to Ovar — circular route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is approximately 37.1 km long and typically takes around 2 hours and 9 minutes to complete at a moderate pace. This duration does not include stops for sightseeing or refreshments.

faqs.question: Is the From Murtosa to Ovar — circular route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally considered to have a moderate difficulty level, making it suitable for most skill levels, including families with some cycling experience. The terrain is predominantly flat, especially along the Ria de Aveiro, with minimal elevation changes. While some segments might require good fitness, the overall gentle nature makes it accessible.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the From Murtosa to Ovar — circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is mostly flat, particularly as it follows the banks of the Ria de Aveiro. Approximately 61% of the route is paved, including quiet roads, regular roads, and dedicated cycleways. You will also encounter some unpaved sections, with about 6% consisting of gravel paths.

faqs.question: Where does the From Murtosa to Ovar — circular route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: As a circular route connecting Murtosa and Ovar, you can start from either town. Both Murtosa and Ovar offer various public parking options. For instance, in Murtosa, areas near the Saldida Park or the town center are good starting points with available parking. In Ovar, parking can be found near the Cais do Carregal or within the urban areas.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse and picturesque landscape. You'll enjoy extensive views of the Ria de Aveiro and its lagoon system, pass through serene forest paths, and experience a blend of natural landscapes and urban areas. Ovar is particularly notable for its vibrant, tiled building facades, earning it the nickname 'City Museum of the Tile'. You'll also cycle through agricultural areas, like those in Bunheiro parish.

faqs.question: Are there any cultural or historical points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The route is rich in cultural and historical sites. Consider detours to visit the Custódio Prato House-Museum, and the Chapels of São Gonçalo, São Simão, and São Silvestre near Bunheiro. In Murtosa, the COMUR-Museu Municipal, which focuses on the eel canning industry, is a recommended stop. Ovar's unique tiled architecture is also a cultural highlight.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the From Murtosa to Ovar — circular route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the spring (April to June) and early autumn (September to October). During these months, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the heavier rains of winter. This allows for comfortable cycling and clear views of the Ria de Aveiro.

faqs.question: Is the From Murtosa to Ovar — circular route part of a larger cycling network?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant portion of this route, specifically between Cais do Carregal (Ovar) and Parque da Saldida (Murtosa), coincides with Stage 3 of the Grande Rota da Ria de Aveiro (GR57), also known as the 'Percurso Dourado' (Golden Route) for cycling. This indicates it's a well-established and recognized part of the regional cycling network.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route connects the towns of Murtosa and Ovar, you will find various cafes, restaurants, and shops in both urban centers where you can stop for refreshments, meals, or to refill water bottles. There may also be smaller establishments in villages along the way, particularly in Bunheiro parish.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate touring bicycle route, we recommend bringing your bicycle (or renting one), a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water, snacks, sun protection (sunscreen, sunglasses), a basic repair kit, and a fully charged phone. Given the cultural stops, a camera might also be a good idea.

faqs.question: Is the From Murtosa to Ovar — circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas and dedicated cycle paths, specific regulations for dogs on bicycles or in certain areas may vary. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they are comfortable with cycling. Always clean up after your pet. Check local municipal guidelines for any specific restrictions in Murtosa or Ovar.

faqs.question: Are there public transport options to reach the starting points of the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Murtosa and Ovar are accessible by public transport. Ovar has a train station on the Linha do Norte, connecting it to major cities like Porto and Aveiro. Murtosa can be reached by regional buses. This allows for flexible travel arrangements to begin your cycling adventure.

waypoints.name: Road Between Cornfields in Ria de Aveiro
